notes: Crashes with Proton 4.2-3. Goes to a black screen with my custom version of Proton based on WINE 4.7
title: Yakuza Kiwami 2

notes: Not much to report, it crashes immediately.
title: Yakuza Kiwami 2

notes: Game starts, flashes a black screen, exits. ESYNC changes nothing, PROTON_USE_WINED3D changes nothing, PROTON_NO_D3D11 causes game to start with no window at all and you have to kill the process.
title: Yakuza Kiwami 2

notes: Shows black screen on launch and crashes but if you press some buttons then SEGA logo appears and everything goes fine (but I can't say for sure that it's pressing  buttons allows to launch the game). When changing AA to SMAA or the whole graphic preset, game crashes. It also has some minor artifacts but they're ok. Performance is a bit too low, I would expect more comparing to some other games that I tried but I guess it's because of my processor and the fact that I don't have dual-channel memory. Controller with xinput works fine. Pretty much playable for me.
title: Yakuza Kiwami 2

notes: Immediate crash was fixed after verifying game files. On default high settings for my computer some framedrops from doing heats moves and during karaoke songs. tested some mini games(Virtua Fighter 2 and Virtual On: constant 60 fps) and so far they work. game runs currently at 30.
title: Yakuza Kiwami 2

notes: The game has some issues to start, as reported by another user. You can't use alt+tab, change graphical settings related to resolution (resolution, render scaling, window mode, shadow quality), antialiasing mode and presets, otherwise the game crashes and you need to delete the pfx folder. If you can live with these problems the game runs good with decent performances. I'm running it at 35fps with medium settings at 1080p, if you want to reach 60fps you need a quad core CPU with fast RAM (even on Windows).
title: Yakuza Kiwami 2

notes:
After disabling Steam Overlay for the game (it's the key to get it working) and tweaking the settings from the INI (otherwise it immediately crash if done ingame) the game is generically working fine, but only under Proton from Valve. I have tried to use Lutris and various version of Wine, but I never achieved to get ingame, always crash at the boot screen. The only other tweak necessary is the gamma, that need to be bumped up to being able to see the game.

As far as my limited gameplay got me, I don't see any graphical glitches (apart maybe for the antialiasing that's is not enabling, for me), however the game still show random boot problems that usually is fixed rebooting Steam and/or killing all associated Wine processes and the impossibility of minimizing the game (but I haven't tried that on Windows, so I don't know if that is an issue of the game or Wine); for this reason I'll give it a bronze status.
title: Yakuza Kiwami 2

notes: Keep launching the game and it will eventually launch. It takes me somewhere about 4 attempts. I played 22 hours and have just finished the main story and Majima's one without any crash or different problem. You don't need any tweaks, the game runs pretty good out of the box. Your the only enemy apart from launching is the game's settings for graphics because some of a features can crash the game. There's only one minor artifact with Kiryu's facial hairs but you won't even notice that one. There's also some strange noise with SSAO but I think it's the whole port bug. So I'm giving it gold.
title: Yakuza Kiwami 2

notes: Crashed when being sent to background, doesn't start all the time, Cut scenes are jittery but watchable, game play has no issues.
title: Yakuza Kiwami 2

notes: Starts and crashes after 5 seconds without any error message.
title: Yakuza Kiwami 2

notes: Can now go in game provided you're using the latest DXVK. Crashes immediately if I have a wireless 360 controller already connected, however launching the game without one and then connecting it after will work fine. No issues changing in-game settings, audio works fine. Only complaint really is the performance, on Windows pretty constant 60FPS with everything maxed (except using FXAA instead of SSAA), with Proton it dips down to ~25 in certain areas and is just stuttery overall. It's decently playable however.
title: Yakuza Kiwami 2

notes:
Performance (default settings, 1080p) is okay most of the time, some times in the city (possibly loading in new parts?) FPS go down and I also often have FPS dips when activating heat actions. 
Starting the game with a gamepad (DS4 connected via USB) crashes the game. Plugging it in after starting the game works (shows Xbox button faces instead of PS).
Changing graphics settings ingame make the game crash, but changing the .ini file in the prefix (steamapps/compatdata/927380/pfx/drive_c/users/steamuser/Application Data/Sega/YakuzaKiwami2/) works fine.
title: Yakuza Kiwami 2

notes:
Download the last DXVK 1.2.1 from github and paste the dll's on the game folder to get in working. If not the game will crash at start up. The last DXVK update also fixes the crash changing game graphics and with the joystick connected. 
Performance is decent but when you enable SSAA it runs very bad, this settings also have performance problems in Yakuza 0 with VEGA/Radeon on Windows. You can tweak the render scale on the .ini and try different values like 1.2 / 1.3 instead of 2.0 that is too aggressive
title: Yakuza Kiwami 2

notes: I'm having some issues with gamma and v-sync settings, otherwise it runs pretty good, 40-50 fps on linux when I would get 50-60 fps on Windows.
title: Yakuza Kiwami 2

notes: Game launches consistently. All settings at max except AA at SMAA and motion blur off - almost identical to how I play it on Windows with nearly identical performance except Render Scale should be reduced to 75% as opposed to 100%. There are slowdowns when performing the spam-Y/Triangle attacks in Extreme Heat Mode , and when loading new parts of the map. As with Windows, Sotenbori has better performance than Kamurocho. Cabaret Club Grand Prix, Majima Construction, Karaoke, Virtua-On, Virtua-Fighter 2.1 and Toylets work perfectly well. The game generally stays around 60fps in my tests. No crashes yet. Cutscenes can be a little choppy because of the varying framerate. It's perfectly playable though, and I'm sure with the right hardware it runs great!
title: Yakuza Kiwami 2

notes:
Platinum doesn't exactly describe this game - no GPU lockups on my GTX 1070, but there were some instances of the game failing to start, or crashing during gameplay. I expect these issues would happen on Windows as well due to the low quality nature of this PC port.

Otherwise, the game worked well - after setting my CPU governor to Performance as with the other Yakuza games, I would never dip below 50 FPS on the highest settings, excluding anti-aliasing. I used SMAA during my entire playthrough as SSAA 2x would dip me down to about 20-25 FPS.

The biggest issue, however, is to do with Heat Attacks. When performing a Heat Attack, the framerate can dip to as low as 20 FPS for the duration of the attack. I found the cause to be the small droplets of blood spawned from the enemies during the attack, and setting the blood to "Mild" in the options basically removed these frame drops entirely. I played for 52 hours and beat the main story and over 2/3 of the substories. Highly recommended.
title: Yakuza Kiwami 2

notes: Playing @ 1440p with all settings maxed out except AA set to SMAA. Anything higher than that causes huge frame drops. Windows 10 does the same. Game never drops below 60 fps and have had no issues. Make sure CPU governor is set to performance.
title: Yakuza Kiwami 2

notes: Doesn't start at all. Not even a window is opened. Using the latest Proton version and all.
title: Yakuza Kiwami 2

notes: Game launches every time for me. Render scale had to be set to 75%, and AA had to be set to FXAA (this is due a bug, also present on Windows). Everything else can be played at max, so long as the CPU Governor is set to performance. These settings, for me, are the exact same as how I played on Windows with near 1:1 performance. I capped the FPS at 60 as added in the latest patch just for preference and to take some load off the GPU. Heat Actions have frame drops to 10fps, but setting the blood to mild fixed this. Played through the whole game, including the Majima saga - 70 hours on Linux, not a single crash.
title: Yakuza Kiwami 2

notes:
The game runs great with no errors. However, there is a noticeable fps drop in Heat actions and in game fps in general. This I think due to the new engine used in game.
Also worth mentioning that i have to launch the game from the shortcut and not steam, otherwise it doesn't start :/
title: Yakuza Kiwami 2

notes: Sometimes does not start and I have to kill process manually, otherwise it's working perfectly fine.
title: Yakuza Kiwami 2

notes: Runs pretty much as well as it does on Windows, except the render scale has to be set to at least 75% (though I got a stable 60 with up to 85% render scale). Game will be a bit slow to start up as on Windows, meaning for the first 30-45 seconds in game you may have a bit of stutter and framerate fluctuation. Let your GPU warm up and you should be good. I recommend capping the framerate at 60fps in the settings (added with a semi-recent patch). Also, there'll be frame drops in battles if you have the "Blood Level" in the game settings set to "Normal" - Turn it down to "Mild" (the only other option) and that'll fix it. Haven't had a crash or boot issue ever since about Proton 4.2-9, and I can run the game directly through Steam. No minigame issues to report either.
title: Yakuza Kiwami 2

notes: Game runs at 60fps (capped) with everything at max except SMAA and Render Scale at 75% (SSAO doesn't work because it sets the render scale to 100%). This is to be expected on my hardware, which is about two years behind what is recommended. It's a miracle the game runs at all. With Valve's ACO Mesa driver, the frame dipping during heat actions has been fixed! Now the game runs buttery smooth on my hardware. If you had something more recent such as a 5700XT or a Radeon VII, you'd probably get a solid 60fps at max settings.
title: Yakuza Kiwami 2
